prompt: The compareTo implementation violates the contract. What is the bug?
choices: The implementation never returns 0 for equal values; compareTo(equal) returns -1 and the reverse also returns -1, violating the antisymmetry contract (sgn(x.compareTo(y)) == -sgn(y.compareTo(x)))
Comparable requires overriding equals() to match compareTo()
int fields must be compared with == before using > in compareTo
explanation: The Comparable contract requires that if x.compareTo(y) < 0 then y.compareTo(x) > 0. Equal values must return 0. Here both a.compareTo(b) and b.compareTo(a) return -1, violating the contract. This causes incorrect sorting and broken TreeSet/TreeMap behavior. Fix: add else if (this.value == other.value) return 0; or simply return Integer.compare(this.value, other.value);
